---
title: "Methodology"
---

# Platforms Evaluated

| Owner           | Platform   | UGC                                                                 | Ads                                                                 |
|-----------------|------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Alphabet Inc.   | YouTube    | [YouTube Data API v3](https://developers.google.com/youtube/v3/docs) | [Ads Transparency Center](https://adstransparency.google.com/)       |
| Bluesky PBC     | Bluesky    | [Bluesky Developer APIs](https://docs.bsky.app/)                     | Not Applicable                                                      |
| ByteDance       | TikTok     | [TikTok Developer APIs](https://developers.tiktok.com/)              | [TikTok Commercial Content API](https://developers.tiktok.com/products/commercial-content-api) |
| Discord Inc.    | Discord    | [Discord API](https://discord.com/developers/docs/intro)             | Not Available                                                       |
| Kuaishou        | Kwai       | [Kuaishou Open Platform API](https://open.kuaishou.com/platform/openApi) | Not Available                                                   |
| Meta            | Facebook   | [Meta Content Library API](https://developers.facebook.com/docs/content-library-and-api/content-library-api/) | [Meta Ad Library API](https://www.facebook.com/ads/library/api/) |
| Meta            | Instagram  | [Meta Content Library API](https://developers.facebook.com/docs/content-library-and-api/content-library-api/) | [Meta Ad Library API](https://www.facebook.com/ads/library/api/) |
| Meta            | Threads    | [Threads API](https://developers.facebook.com/docs/threads-api)      | Not Available                                                       |
| Meta            | WhatsApp   | [WhatsApp Business Platform API](https://developers.facebook.com/docs/whatsapp) | Not Available                                           |
| Microsoft       | LinkedIn   | [LinkedIn Developer APIs](https://learn.microsoft.com/linkedin/)     | [LinkedIn Marketing API](https://learn.microsoft.com/linkedin/marketing/) |
| Pinterest       | Pinterest  | [Pinterest API v5](https://developers.pinterest.com/docs/api/v5/)    | [Pinterest Ads API](https://developers.pinterest.com/docs/redoc/ads/) |
| Reddit Inc.     | Reddit     | [Reddit API](https://www.reddit.com/dev/api/)                        | [Reddit Ads API](https://ads-api.reddit.com/docs/)                  |
| Snap Inc.       | Snapchat   | [Snapchat Public Profile API](https://developers.snap.com/api/public-profile-api) | [Snap Marketing API](https://developers.snap.com/api/marketing-api) |
| Telegram        | Telegram   | [Telegram Bot API](https://core.telegram.org/bots/api)               | Not Available                                                       |
| X Corp.         | X          | [X API](https://developer.x.com/en/docs/x-api)                       | [EU Ads Transparency Center](https://ads.x.com/transparency)        |

# Scoring Methodology

# UGC

**Special Criteria Score (75% weight):**

$$
S_{SC} = 75 \sum_{i=1}^{4} w_i \cdot SC_i
$$

where $w_1 = w_2 = w_3 = 0.30$ and $w_4 = 0.10$

**Other Criteria Score (25% weight):**

$$
S_{OC} = 25 \cdot \frac{\sum_{j=1}^{n} OC_j}{\text{number of applicable questions}}
$$

where the denominator represents the number of questions for which an answer is applicable.

**Total Score:**

$$
\text{Total Score} = S_{SC} + S_{OC}
$$

# Ads

**Special Criteria Score (75% weight):**

$$
S_{SC} = 75 \sum_{i=1}^{3} w_i \cdot SC_i
$$

where $w_1 = 0.50$, $w_2 = 0.30$, and $w_3 = 0.20$

**Other Criteria Score (25% weight):**

$$
S_{OC} = 25 \cdot \frac{\sum_{j=1}^{n} OC_j}{\text{number of applicable questions}}
$$

where the denominator represents the number of questions for which an answer is applicable.


**Total Score:**

$$
\text{Total Score} = S_{SC} + S_{OC}
$$

**Scoring Rules:**

- Special Criteria: $SC_i \in \{0, 0.5, 1\}$ where 0.5 is awarded for partial ad data
- Other Criteria: $OC_j \in \{0, 0.5, 1\}$ where 0.5 is awarded when feature is available through only API or GUI, and 1.0 when available through both

# Questionnaires

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis

import yaml
from pathlib import Path

def format_category_title(category):
    return category.replace('_', ' ').title()

def display_questions(yaml_file, title):
    print(f"\n## {title}\n")

    with open(yaml_file, 'r') as f:
        data = yaml.safe_load(f)

    for category, questions in data.items():
        print(f"\n### {format_category_title(category)}\n")

        for q in questions:
            print(f"**{q['code']}**: {q['title']}\n")
            print(f"*Description*: {q['description']}\n")
            print(f"*Available answers*:\n")
            for answer in q['answers']:
                print(f"- {answer['label']} (weight: {answer['weight']})")
            print("\n")

display_questions('../data/2025/questions_ugc_2025.yml', 'User-Generated Content (UGC) Questions')
display_questions('../data/2025/questions_ads_2025.yml', 'Advertising (Ads) Questions')
```
